questions.question: What are the key properties that a good election algorithm should satisfy?
expectedPoints: Safety: At most one leader is elected at any time
Liveness: Eventually a leader is elected if no failures occur
Fault Tolerance: Works correctly despite process failures
Efficiency: Uses minimal messages and time

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Bully Algorithm and Ring Algorithm in terms of message complexity, fault tolerance, and implementation requirements.
expectedPoints: Bully Algorithm: O(n²) message complexity, requires complete process knowledge, elects highest-ID process
Ring Algorithm: O(n) message complexity, requires logical ring structure, handles multiple initiations
Fault tolerance: Both handle process failures but in different ways
Implementation: Bully needs all-to-all communication, Ring needs circular topology
Practical considerations for each algorithm in real-world systems
